plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-375 RP12.P04.M03.S21-RP12.P04.M05.S16 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-375 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP12.P04 workflow permission slice: the M03 Primary Implementation Slice tail (the two remaining rows), the full M04 Secondary Workflow Slice cycle (twenty-two rows), and the M05 Permission And Audit Binding head (the first sixteen rows) as three descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H12), Claude review packet (C12), and CP00-376 handoff, without opening billing, proforma, invoice, or write-down runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-375 is a descriptor-only RP12 billing-core closeout pack (40 units, RP12.P04.M03.S21-RP12.P04.M05.S16, risk B) covering the M03 Primary Implementation Slice tail (2 rows), full M04 Secondary Workflow Slice (22 rows), and M05 Permission And Audit Binding head (16 rows). Read-only inspection confirms the pack-level posture is correctly conservative: production_ready is FALSE across manifest (status implemented_pending_review, runtime_ready false) and command-evidence (production_ready false), with no claim of production readiness or enterprise trust from local validation. Scope, unit_count, range, deliverable distribution {ui:18,implementation:11,claude_review:3,security_audit:4,fixture:2,test:1,hermes_evidence:1}=40, and micro/micro-title counts are mutually consistent across the live closeout-pack-plan.json entry, review baseline, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, billing-core-contract.json current_pack/cp375_requirements/cp375_no_write_attestation, registry binding, and model.test.js. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are sound: freezeCp375Result and the inherited CP364->CP375 attestation chain set all runtime/write/leak flags false (promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false, writes_product_state:false), and the new code is pure frozen-object construction with no I/O, DB, network, or runtime dispatch. Claude is correctly scoped as a read-only independent reviewer (Read,Grep,Glob; claude_is_final_approval:false; human final approval still required), and Hermes patterns appear only as descriptor-only review packet/receipt evidence (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false), not product runtime. CP178+ review hardening is encoded via inherited forbidden_review_evidence (auth-failure, zero-byte, malformed-JSON, fenced-JSON, tool-call-shaped, missing-field, final-approval-claim, source-mutation, production-enterprise-trust blockers); the readiness auth preflight passed with valid JSON and non-zero stdout. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, raw-output.json, review-receipt.json) are correctly absent at this pre-review stage and are gated to be produced after this review per the hardening sequence. Only two minor, non-blocking P3 informational notes were found; no P0/P1/P2 issues. This is an independent reviewer recommendation and not final approval; human final approval remains required for any enterprise trust claim.

findings.title: Hermes evidence field name 'production_ready_candidate' could be misread as a runtime production-readiness assertion
findings.details: createBillingCoreCp375HermesEvidencePacket sets production_ready_candidate: coverage.valid && slice.valid. The field reflects only descriptor coverage and slice validity, not runtime production readiness. All authoritative pack-level signals correctly report the pack is NOT production ready (manifest.json production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status implemented_pending_review; command-evidence.json production_ready=false). The candidate flag does not propagate any production_ready=true claim to the pack or goal level, so there is no overclaim; this is purely a naming-clarity observation.
findings.recommendation: Optionally rename to descriptor_validity_candidate (or add an inline note) to make explicit that the flag is descriptor-validity only and distinct from pack-level production_ready. No action required for closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; verified not to overclaim at pack or goal level; does not block pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: Inert 'documentation_entry' key in BILLING_CORE_CP375_ROW_EXTRAS never merges into any row
findings.details: BILLING_CORE_CP375_ROW_EXTRAS adds a documentation_entry key whose value is an object, but billingCoreRowKey produces row keys such as 'state_snapshot' / 'ui_surface_inventory'; no planned CP00-375 row title maps to the key 'documentation_entry'. The ROW_EXTRAS lookup (BILLING_CORE_CP375_ROW_EXTRAS[key] ?? {}) therefore never applies this entry to a row. The effective documentation linkage is the descriptor-level documentation_entry: 'packages/billing/README.md#cp00-375' (service.js:1528), and the README #cp00-375 anchor exists. This matches the established, test-passing pattern from prior packs and has no functional impact.
findings.recommendation: Optionally remove the unused ROW_EXTRAS documentation_entry key for clarity, or leave as-is to preserve cross-pack pattern symmetry. No action required.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational/cosmetic; no functional impact; does not block pack or goal closeout.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-375 billing core p04 workflow permission slice descriptor pack
